summary: _cfg now falls back to the shared Xylem session pointer (~/.xylem/active_project.json) when AGENTSYNC_REPO is unset, so agentsync coordinates whichever project the session is in instead of requiring a pinned repo.
problem: agentsync hard-required AGENTSYNC_REPO, so the Xylem installer pinned it to the install-time $PROJECT_DIR and every session coordinated that one frozen repo. Part of Xylem-stack audit fix 1b (make context-keeper and agentsync follow the session like cambium does).
why_chosen: _cfg already reads AGENTSYNC_REPO from the env per call, so adding `or _xylem_session_project()` is a one-line, low-risk change. The pointer is the same shared file context-keeper and the Xylem hook use, honored only when the explicit env pin is absent, so existing pinned setups are unchanged. Agent id still comes from AGENTSYNC_AGENT_ID.
tradeoffs: If neither the env nor the pointer resolves (session not in a project), _cfg raises ConfigError as before — agentsync needs a repo. _log still reads AGENTSYNC_REPO directly and silently no-ops when unpinned, acceptable for a breadcrumb log.
